retrieved_docs.title: Overview of Periodontal Surgical Procedures -- Indications -- Crown Exposure
retrieved_docs.content: Altered passive eruption ("gummy smile") with bone approximating or covering the cementoenamel junction, resulting in an excess gingival display; no restoration is needed

retrieved_docs.title: Overview of Periodontal Surgical Procedures -- Technique or Treatment -- Crown Exposure
retrieved_docs.content: Crown exposure is similar to crown lengthening, but it is done in a healthy environment where restoration is unnecessary, and the goal is to expose the anatomic crown fully. Typically, this procedure is done in the anterior maxilla for improved aesthetics in cases of a gummy smile caused by the altered passive eruption. In altered passive eruption, alveolar bone encroaches or covers the cementoenamel junction after complete facial growth. [54] This procedure is also known as aesthetic crown lengthening. For aesthetic crown lengthening, the Chu proportion gauge and laboratory-fabricated surgery guides can help guide bone removal for improved outcomes. [58] [59]

retrieved_docs.title: Overview of Periodontal Surgical Procedures -- Indications -- Gingivectomy
retrieved_docs.content: Altered passive eruption ("gummy smile") with excess gingiva covering tooth but crestal bone remaining 2 mm apical to the cementoenamel junction Removal of genetic or medication-induced gingival enlargement Removal of pigmented gingiva (racial pigmentation tends to return long-term)

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Treatment / Management -- Preadolescents
retrieved_docs.content: Use of tongue blade: This method can correct the development of crossbite. A tongue blade resembles a flat ice cream stick placed inside the mouth, contacting the erupting tooth in a crossbite on its palatal side. During the slight closure of the jaws, the opposing side of the tooth comes in contact with the labial aspect of the opposing mandibular tooth. Light forces generated during this period might help the tooth attain a better position. Catalan’s appliance or lower inclined plane: The lower inclined plane is constructed at an angle of 45 to the maxillary occlusal plane and can be cemented on lower incisors. Face masks and Rapid maxillary expansion: This method can be used when a skeletal-transverse deficiency occurs in the maxilla Frankel III appliance: Can be used to correct a developing Class III skeletal malocclusion. Chin cup appliance: It can be used to redirect the growth of a prognathic mandible.

retrieved_docs.content: The normal interlabial distance at rest ranges from 0 to 4 mm. [3] An increased interlabial gap can be caused by short lips, dentoalveolar extrusion, or VME, and clinicians should investigate and identify which is the cause of the discrepancy. [3] [5] Smile Line

retrieved_docs.title: Excessive Gingival Display -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Crown lengthening with or without bony resection, called gingivectomy, removes the excessive gingival tissue and reinserts the attachment apparatus. [16] The decision of performing only a gingivectomy or a gingivectomy with osseous resection depends on the amount of biological width. A gingivectomy is sufficient to resolve a gummy smile when there are adequate osseous levels and attached gingiva, and the gingival tissue from bone to the gingival crest is more than 3 mm. [16] But, a full-thickness periodontal flap in combination with osteotomy is indicated when the osseous level is near the cementoenamel junction - a gingivectomy alone disrupts the biologic width. [17] Lip Repositioning Surgery

retrieved_docs.title: Excessive Gingival Display -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Altered passive eruption is the gingival tissue's incapacity to migrate apically past stage 2. [5] Teeth appear short and square because the gingival tissues are coronal to the CEJ. [1] Vital elements to diagnosing altered passive eruption include ruling out a hypermobile lip and checking the CEJ and alveolar crest location. The lips should be examined while resting and smiling. In a hyperactive lip, a translational movement from rest can be up to 10 mm, while in a normal lip activity, it is between 6 and 8 mm. [5] In altered passive eruption, the CEJ can be found at up to 10 mm apical to the free gingival margin. [5] The alveolar crest level is the same as in a healthy condition: at the level of 1 to 2 mm apical to the CEJ. [5] Probing to the bone and a parallel x-ray determine the alveolar crest level. [6] [7] [8] Anterior Dentoalveolar Extrusion

retrieved_docs.title: Excessive Gingival Display -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The smile line is defined as the upper lip position concerning the upper incisors and gingiva during a natural full smile. [9] A standard smile line shows 75% to 100% of the crowns with the interproximal gingiva. [1] A low smile line shows less than 75% of the crown, usually a male feature. [15] A high smile line (gummy smile) shows the whole crown and an excessive amount of gingiva, usually a female characteristic. [1] [15] Periodontal Examination

retrieved_docs.content: Residual pocketing at a distal or mesial tooth surface that faces excessively thick gingival tissue Typically at the most distal tooth of the arch, adjacent to thick tissue of the maxillary tuberosity or the retromolar pad

retrieved_docs.title: Facial Nerve Trauma -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Frontal: elevate brows, furrow brows (scowl). Zygomatic: close eyes gently (or watch for natural blinks), close eyes tightly - the distinction between the ability to close the eye with normal effort vs. with full effort is the difference between House-Brackmann grades II and III. Buccal: smile, pucker lips. Marginal mandibular: depress lower lip to show bottom teeth. Cervical: contract platysma. Overall: look for asymmetry at rest, particularly in brow height, lower eyelid position, nasolabial fold depth and orientation, the position of the upper lip philtrum, and the position of the oral commissures.

retrieved_docs.title: Excessive Gingival Display -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: An excessive gingival display is an aesthetic concern that interferes with patients’ self-esteem and psychological status. [17] Gummy smiles can be a cause of embarrassment, leading to patients hiding or controlling their smiles. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 5 -- Community 2925
retrieved_docs.content: The condition described as “CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN” is clinically associated with “ولادة قبل الأوان” (preterm birth). This relationship suggests that when a caesarean section scar cannot be classified, there may be an increased likelihood of delivering before term, highlighting the importance of careful obstetric monitoring and management in patients with indeterminate scar types.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2820
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) is clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم, a condition in which the uterine cervix is unable to maintain sufficient closure during pregnancy, leading to an increased risk of preterm birth or pregnancy loss. The cerclage technique involves placing a suturing material around the cervix to reinforce its structural integrity, thereby preventing premature cervical dilation. This intervention is employed as a therapeutic measure to manage cervical insufficiency and improve obstetric outcomes for affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 4 -- Community 2769
retrieved_docs.content: Low transverse cesarean section is clinically associated with تمزق الأغشية الباكر (premature rupture of membranes), indicating that this surgical approach to delivery may be linked to an increased occurrence of early membrane rupture, a factor that clinicians must consider when planning obstetric care and managing potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1702
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN and High risk are both clinically associated with cesarean section, indicating that an unidentified type of cesarean scar and a high‑risk patient status are relevant factors in the consideration, planning, and postoperative management of cesarean deliveries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1845
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with cesarean section, indicating that patients who have undergone a cesarean delivery may present with a scar of unspecified type, a factor that clinicians should consider when evaluating postoperative healing, potential complications, and future obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 5 -- Community 2926
retrieved_docs.content: Cesarean section is clinically associated with the low transverse cesarean section technique, and the low transverse cesarean section is in turn clinically associated with early membrane rupture (تمزق الأغشية الباكر). This relationship highlights that the specific low transverse incision used during a cesarean delivery may be linked to an increased likelihood of premature rupture of the fetal membranes, a factor that clinicians consider when planning surgical approaches and managing obstetric risk.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 1021
retrieved_docs.content: organizes general obstetric procedure categories (إجراء توليدي, Antepartum Procedures, Obstetrics incision) and specific clinical actions—ranging from diagnostic sampling and membrane management to fetal stimulation, reduction of multiple gestations, and surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancies—providing a comprehensive framework for coordinating obstetric care across the ante‑partum, intrapartum, and postpartum periods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1447
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) and REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR are both clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم. قصور عنق الرحم, also known as cervical insufficiency, is a condition in which the cervix is unable to maintain its structural integrity during pregnancy, increasing the risk of premature dilation and preterm delivery. The cerclage procedure involves placing a suturing stitch around the cervix to reinforce it and prevent premature opening, thereby directly addressing the mechanical weakness characteristic of قصور عنق الرحم. Additionally, a poor reproductive history—such as prior miscarriages, preterm births, or other adverse pregnancy outcomes—has been linked to a higher likelihood of developing قصور عنق الرحم, indicating that past obstetric complications can serve as a clinical indicator for this cervical disorder. Recognizing both the therapeutic role of cerclage and the predictive value of reproductive history is essential for early identification and management of patients at risk for قصور عنق الرحم.

retrieved_docs.content: severe delivery procedures, and immediate post‑delivery care. Parent classes include Female Genital and Obstetric Procedures, Other Procedures Inducing or Assisting Delivery, and Other Procedures to Assist Delivery. Specific members listed are cesarean hysterectomy, reduction of multiple pregnancy, cervical cerclage (and removal of cerclage), labor induction (medical, surgical, stabilizing), artificial rupture of membranes, obstetrics incision (grouping cardiac puncture, amniocentesis, perineal incision), preventive incision related to episiotomy, and episiotomy itself. Fetal Monitoring (مراقبة الجنين) is positioned as a child of Obstetric Surgical Procedures and as an inverse‑isa of Procedure on Organ; it links to uterine procedures, placental operations, amnion assessment, and serves as the direct monitoring site within the female reproductive organs. Continuous fetal monitoring is integral to routine obstetric care, which also includes ante‑partum management, vaginal birth
